role.name: Developer
role.description: Fix bugs, manage code safely, and survive production pressure.

chapters.title: The Login Button Disaster
chapters.story: Monday morning. A tiny login bug arrives. The senior says, “Bas ek small fix hai.” Famous last words. The last user cannot login, and production is watching you like a strict school principal.
chapters.task: Choose the safest way to fix the login bug.

outcome.title: Production Samosa Attack
outcome.description: The login button starts ordering samosas for every user. Client logs in and receives chutney instead of dashboard.
outcome.moralLesson: Never touch production without branch, testing, and review.
outcome.consequenceSummary: Because you changed production directly, a regression risk is now active and a rollback cleanup mission is unlocked.

debrief.whatWentWell: You reacted quickly to a visible bug.
debrief.whatWasMissed: You skipped branch discipline, test evidence, and review.
debrief.realWorldPrinciple: Production fixes need controlled rollout, verification, and rollback readiness.

delayedConsequenceMessages: Senior Dev will challenge your next fix plan because you skipped testing.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Never touch production without branch, testing, and review.

choices.text: Debug locally, test edge cases, then raise PR

outcome.title: Responsible Developer Ending
outcome.description: You fix the loop condition, test first and last user, create a clean commit, and raise PR. Senior says, “Good. Aaj tumne meeting bachayi.”
outcome.consequenceSummary: Your disciplined PR lowers regression risk and improves stakeholder confidence.

debrief.whatWentWell: You reproduced, fixed, tested, and communicated through a PR.
debrief.whatWasMissed: Nothing major. Keep documenting edge cases.
debrief.realWorldPrinciple: Good engineering reduces invisible future work.

delayedConsequenceMessages: Your documented fix gives the team evidence for the next production incident.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: acceptable start, but explain the trade-off and document what you checked.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.

choices.text: Ignore the ticket and pretend you are in focus mode

outcome.title: Silent Ninja Ending
outcome.description: Client escalates. Manager asks for update. You say, “I was analyzing.” Everyone hears: “I forgot.”
outcome.consequenceSummary: The issue remains unresolved and your ownership reputation drops.

debrief.whatWentWell: You avoided making the bug worse directly.
debrief.whatWasMissed: No update, no evidence, and no ownership.
debrief.realWorldPrinciple: Silence creates escalation faster than a bug does.

delayedConsequenceMessages: The team will remember that you went silent during the login incident.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.

miniGame.title: Patch the Login Button
miniGame.instructions: Pick the safest code fix for the login button without creating a second disaster.
miniGame.prompt: The login API succeeds, but the UI still shows loading forever. What code change fixes the state update properly?
miniGame.hint: The UI needs to know that loading has finished after the async call returns.

options.text: Set isLoading = false inside setState after the API response is handled.
options.helperText: Updates UI state after the async operation.

options.text: Tell users to restart the app after every login.
Delete the loading spinner widget from the screen.
options.helperText: The bug disappears visually, but the app is still confused.

miniGame.successMessage: Clean fix! The login button stopped spinning like a ceiling fan during a power cut.
miniGame.failureMessage: Oops. The spinner has applied for permanent residency on the login screen. QA has named it Ramesh.

failureConsequence.consequenceSummary: The wrong patch choice increases cleanup pressure before the story continues.

debrief.whatWasMissed: The UI state bug still needs a controlled fix.
debrief.realWorldPrinciple: A hidden state bug is still a bug.

successConsequence.consequenceSummary: The patch challenge improves technical confidence before the decision.

dialogues.speaker: Narrator
dialogues.text: Monday morning. The login button is spinning forever, and the client has already typed “urgent” with three extra exclamation marks.

audio.subtitle: Monday morning. The login button is spinning forever, and the client has already typed “urgent”

dialogues.speaker: Senior Dev
dialogues.text: Small fix hai. But remember: small fixes are where production disasters wear tiny shoes.

audio.subtitle: Small fix hai. But remember: small fixes are where production disasters wear tiny shoes.

dialogues.speaker: Project Manager
dialogues.text: Client is waiting. Do we patch directly, raise a PR, or pretend the spinner is a meditation feature?

audio.subtitle: Client is waiting. Do we patch directly, raise a PR, or pretend the spinner is a meditation feat

dialogues.speaker: You
dialogues.text: This choice will affect trust, chaos, and whether a rollback cleanup mission appears later.

audio.subtitle: This choice will affect trust, chaos, and whether a rollback cleanup mission appears later.

storyFlags.label: Mentor warned about shortcuts
storyFlags.description: Senior developer remembers that testing was skipped under pressure.

storyFlags.label: Evidence-based fix
storyFlags.description: The player documented findings before changing production behavior.

storyFlags.label: Avoided ownership
storyFlags.description: The player tried to disappear instead of owning the incident.

chapters.learningObjective: Practice bug triage workflow for a Developer situation involving debugging, git discipline, testing.
safetyGuardrails: Do not hotfix production without review, test evidence, and rollback plan.
chapters.practicalTakeaway: Use the bug triage workflow to balance professional judgment with constraints instead of guessing under pressure.
chapters.safeExplanation: Software simulation: protect users by using test evidence, review, rollback planning, and secure handling of data. This chapter is educational and does not replace qualified professional review.
chapters.mentorFeedback: Senior Dev Mentor says: At beginner level, show your evidence, explain the trade-off, and follow the bug triage workflow before choosing speed.

guardrails: Do not hotfix production without review, test evidence, and rollback plan.
safetyReview.notes: Phase 23 metadata seed. Requires human review before production publishing changes.

adaptiveDialogueInjections.speaker: Senior Dev
adaptiveDialogueInjections.text: Before you touch production again, remember your shortcut pattern: evidence first, drama later.

adaptiveDialogueInjections.speaker: Senior Dev
adaptiveDialogueInjections.text: You have been handling discipline well, so this time I expect a rollback plan, not just a fix.

chapters.title: The API Timeout Panic
chapters.story: The app works in office Wi-Fi, but users on mobile data see a spinner longer than a family wedding video. The manager says, “Just increase timeout to unlimited.”
chapters.task: Fix the unstable API experience safely.

choices.text: Set timeout to 10 minutes and call it solved

outcome.title: Infinite Spinner Ending
outcome.description: Users wait so long they finish lunch, tea, and existential thinking before the request fails.
outcome.moralLesson: Long timeout hides a problem; it does not solve the experience.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Long timeout hides a problem; it does not solve the experience.

choices.text: Check logs, add proper timeout, retry, loading state, and user message

outcome.title: Stable Network Ending
outcome.description: Failures become understandable, retries are controlled, and users stop blaming the phone company.
outcome.moralLesson: Reliable apps handle failure as carefully as success.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: acceptable start, but explain the trade-off and document what you checked.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Reliable apps handle failure as carefully as success.

choices.text: Blame backend without checking request payload

outcome.title: Backend War Ending
outcome.description: Backend sends proof, QA sends screen recording, and your payload contains customerId: null.
outcome.moralLesson: Debug with evidence before blaming another team.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Debug with evidence before blaming another team.

dialogues.speaker: Senior Dev
dialogues.text: The API timeout is not just a spinner problem. It is a trust problem wearing a loading indicator.

audio.subtitle: The API timeout is not just a spinner problem. It is a trust problem wearing a loading indicator

dialogues.speaker: Manager
dialogues.text: Client is asking whether the app is down or just practicing meditation.

audio.subtitle: Client is asking whether the app is down or just practicing meditation.

dialogues.speaker: Senior Dev
dialogues.text: Last time you touched production before proving the fix. This time, I want evidence before action.

audio.subtitle: Last time you touched production before proving the fix. This time, I want evidence before actio

dialogues.speaker: Senior Dev
dialogues.text: Your notes from the login incident helped. Bring that same discipline to this timeout.

audio.subtitle: Your notes from the login incident helped. Bring that same discipline to this timeout.

dialogues.speaker: Senior Dev
dialogues.text: The team noticed you vanished last time. Today is your chance to rebuild trust.

audio.subtitle: The team noticed you vanished last time. Today is your chance to rebuild trust.

chapters.learningObjective: Practice code review workflow for a Developer situation involving api handling, logs, retries.
safetyGuardrails: Do not hotfix production without review, test evidence, and rollback plan.
chapters.practicalTakeaway: Use the code review workflow to balance professional judgment with constraints instead of guessing under pressure.
chapters.safeExplanation: Software simulation: protect users by using test evidence, review, rollback planning, and secure handling of data. This chapter is educational and does not replace qualified professional review.
chapters.mentorFeedback: Senior Dev Mentor says: At intermediate level, show your evidence, explain the trade-off, and follow the code review workflow before choosing speed.

guardrails: Do not hotfix production without review, test evidence, and rollback plan.

chapters.title: Friday Hotfix Circus
chapters.story: Friday 6:45 PM. A production issue appears. Everyone wants a quick release before dinner. The build machine looks nervous.

outcome.title: Laptop Legend Disaster
outcome.description: Your local config points to staging, release notes say “minor changes,” and production starts speaking test data.
outcome.moralLesson: A hotfix still needs controlled release steps.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: A hotfix still needs controlled release steps.

choices.text: Create hotfix branch, test fix, prepare rollback, and release with approval

outcome.title: Calm Release Ending
outcome.description: The issue is fixed, rollback is ready, and your weekend survives with dignity.
outcome.moralLesson: Urgency increases the need for process.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: acceptable start, but explain the trade-off and document what you checked.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Urgency increases the need for process.

choices.text: Disable the feature without telling anyone

outcome.title: Invisible Feature Ending
outcome.description: The bug is gone because the feature is gone. Client asks if the app has entered meditation mode.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.

chapters.learningObjective: Practice rollback planning workflow for a Developer situation involving release discipline and rollback planning.
safetyGuardrails: Do not hotfix production without review, test evidence, and rollback plan.
chapters.practicalTakeaway: Use the rollback planning workflow to balance professional judgment with constraints instead of guessing under pressure.
chapters.safeExplanation: Software simulation: protect users by using test evidence, review, rollback planning, and secure handling of data. This chapter is educational and does not replace qualified professional review.
chapters.mentorFeedback: Senior Dev Mentor says: At advanced level, show your evidence, explain the trade-off, and follow the rollback planning workflow before choosing speed.

guardrails: Do not hotfix production without review, test evidence, and rollback plan.

chapters.title: Rollback the Samosa Release
chapters.story: The skipped-testing ghost returns. Production users are still seeing weird login behavior, and the client has started using the phrase “business impact” with dangerous calmness.
chapters.task: Choose the safest cleanup action to recover trust.
chapters.professionalLearningPoint: Cleanup work should reduce risk, preserve evidence, and communicate clearly with stakeholders.

choices.text: Rollback to last stable build, document impact, add regression test, and communicate ETA.

outcome.title: Controlled Recovery
outcome.description: The app returns to stable behavior. QA gets a regression test, the PM gets a clear ETA, and the client stops typing in ALL CAPS.
outcome.moralLesson: Good cleanup repairs both the system and trust.
outcome.consequenceSummary: The production regression risk is cleared and stakeholder trust starts recovering.

debrief.whatWentWell: You stabilized first, then fixed with evidence.
debrief.whatWasMissed: The original shortcut still cost time.
debrief.realWorldPrinciple: Rollback is not defeat; it is risk control.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: good judgment. You balanced action with evidence, communication, and professional responsibility.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Good cleanup repairs both the system and trust.

choices.text: Patch again directly because two wrongs might become a framework.

outcome.title: Patch Tornado
outcome.description: The login bug now has cousins. One cousin lives in logout, another in forgot password.
outcome.consequenceSummary: Cleanup remains unresolved and your reliability drops further.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.

choices.text: Blame cache and close laptop slowly.

outcome.title: Cache Baba Excuse
outcome.description: The client clears cache. The bug waves hello again.
outcome.consequenceSummary: Trust drops because the root cause was not addressed.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.

safetyGuardrails: Do not hotfix production without review, test evidence, and rollback plan.
chapters.practicalTakeaway: Use the bug triage workflow to balance professional judgment with constraints instead of guessing under pressure.
chapters.safeExplanation: Software simulation: protect users by using test evidence, review, rollback planning, and secure handling of data. This chapter is educational and does not replace qualified professional review.

chapters.title: Release Readiness Finale
chapters.theme: Mastery check, risk control, stakeholder trust
chapters.story: Final release day arrives. QA, PM, and client are all watching the build pipeline like it is a cricket final.
chapters.professionalLearningPoint: A reliable developer balances speed, evidence, rollback readiness, and communication.

choices.text: Check test evidence, rollback plan, release notes, and stakeholder sign-off.

outcome.title: Release Captain Ending
outcome.description: The release is boring, which is secretly the highest compliment in software.
outcome.moralLesson: Professional releases are calm because preparation absorbs chaos.
outcome.consequenceSummary: Finale completed with a strong professional profile.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: good judgment. You balanced action with evidence, communication, and professional responsibility.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Professional releases are calm because preparation absorbs chaos.

choices.text: Release now and promise to monitor WhatsApp carefully.

outcome.title: Notification Anxiety Ending
outcome.description: Every phone vibration now sounds like production crying.
outcome.moralLesson: Monitoring is not a replacement for readiness.
outcome.consequenceSummary: You finish, but your readiness gap affects the final ending.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Monitoring is not a replacement for readiness.

endingRules.title: Production-Safe Developer
endingRules.description: You protected users and earned mentor trust.

endingRules.title: Relationship Repair Developer
endingRules.description: You learned, but trust still needs rebuilding.
